---
title: My dynamic report
output: html_fragment
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE)

```

```{css}

body {
  font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
  margin: 20px;
}

h2 {
  color: #2c3e50;
}

p {
  line-height: 1.6;
}
```

## ## Analysis of Daily Step Counts and Lifestyle Factors

This dynamic report analyses daily step counts collected through a Google Form. The dataset includes variables such as the number of steps taken each day, the main activity performed, and the weather conditions.

The purpose of this analysis is to explore how different lifestyle factors, such as activity type and weather, are associated with daily step counts.

```{r}
library(tidyverse)

logged_data <- read_csv("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vQvf9MEZcYxYiSSxXHP9iRCQ8fUy5Q1OCGBU8a-ifgLBgczFVg8zpmePe4nKDcqvxZnqXxAGs7Mv9kx/pub?output=csv")

latest_data <- logged_data %>%
  rename(
    timestamp = Timestamp,
    steps = `How many steps did you walk today?`,
    activity = `What was your main activity today?`,
    check_time = `When did you check your step count?`,
    weather = `What was the weather like today?`
  )
```
The average number of steps recorded is `r round(mean(latest_data$steps), 2)`.

The maximum number of steps recorded is `r max(latest_data$steps)`.

```{r}
ggplot(latest_data, aes(x = activity)) +
  geom_bar(fill="#0000FF") +
  labs(
    title = "Main Activity Distribution",
    x = "Activity",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
ggplot(latest_data, aes(x = weather)) +
  geom_bar(fill="#FF0000") +
  labs(
    title = "Weather Distribution",
    x = "Weather",
    y = "Count"
  )
```

The bar chart below shows the distribution of different activity types recorded in the dataset. It helps us understand which activities are most common.

The second bar chart shows the distribution of weather conditions. This allows us to observe how frequently each type of weather occurs in the data.

![](https://c.tenor.com/-qBsG1HwR4oAAAAC/tenor.gif)
